data.content_ref: inline:The problem context explicitly states that the hard set exhibited 'critic recall 1.0, precision 0.125; net fixed-minus-broke 0 — with 4/5 majorities already right, filtering changed nothing.' Target c9386a6c7b3038d7ebaf9f80aed2b6bfd17ad2be03ed64556d0173f0d0085495 claims that the repository's null results are consistent with its theory because 'even with recall=1.0 on the hard set, the flagged candidates were on questions where SC was already right, so no fixable-wrong-majority existed.' This is a fabricated fact. The provided context states the candidate base error was 0.01 on the hard set, meaning there was exactly 1 wrong candidate out of 100 total candidates across the 20 questions. A recall of 1.0 means that single wrong candidate was flagged. The target claims the flagged candidates were on questions where SC was already right, but with 1 wrong candidate flagged and a net fixed-minus-broke of 0, the critic must have also flagged at least 1 correct candidate (yielding precision 0.125). The target invents the specific claim that the flags missed wrong-majority questions entirely to rationalize the null result, misrepresenting the measured data.
